description_long: This object is the forward fuselage section of a commercial passenger airplane, featuring a rounded nose and a cockpit with multiple rectangular windows. The fuselage is largely intact at the front, but the rear is open and appears to be missing, exposing the interior. On one side, there is a large open doorway, likely for passenger entry or cargo loading, and the landing gear strut is visible beneath the fuselage. The surface is painted in a pale, weathered color, with no visible airline markings or livery. The wings and tail section are absent, and the object rests on a rough, uneven ground surface, suggesting it may be a decommissioned or abandoned airframe. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with visible structural details and some debris or detached parts nearby.
description: A weathered, pale-colored forward fuselage of a commercial airplane with cockpit windows, an open doorway, and missing wings and tail. The structure is partially open at the rear and rests on rough ground.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and a detached circular part nearby.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the open doorway, landing gear strut, and the exposed interior at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear-angled view highlighting the open, hollow end of the fuselage and the absence of the tail section.
description_view_3: Oblique front-side view showing the cockpit windows, smooth nose, and the missing rear section.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Wings absent; original configuration not visible.
attribute_tail_design: Tail section absent; original configuration not visible.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Single visible strut, likely part of a tricycle landing gear; other gear units not visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple rectangular windows.
